message:
fix(test): add serial_test to fix flaky env var tests (bd-zxwt)

Tests that modify environment variables (test_terminal_env_apply,
test_terminal_env_no_color, test_with_env_var) were failing
intermittently due to race conditions with parallel test execution.

Added serial_test crate and #[serial] attribute to ensure these
tests run sequentially.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-yb9","title":"Renderable for str ignores Console markup setting","description":"Renderable impl for str always parses markup via render_or_plain and ignores ConsoleOptions.markup. This makes print_renderable(&str) behave differently from Console::print when markup is disabled. Consider honoring options.markup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:10:06.064517230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:53.592328815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:53.592282037Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Renderable for str now checks options.markup and skips markup parsing when disabled","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

All tests pass with deterministic output. Child beads bd-2cyz and bd-cisk closed.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:43.998207824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-1365","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:53.291720395Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:48.662582585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:09.915171991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:50.940799339Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:56.041645930Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","depends_on_id":"bd-5bjf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:46.300688024Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-yzr8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot strategy

- Prefer snapshotting ANSI-stripped output to avoid terminal-dependent differences.
- Use fixed dimensions via `--width/--height` and deterministic seed via `--seed`.
- Snapshot only the most important scenes (hero, dashboard snapshot mode, debug_tools) to keep snapshot maintenance manageable.

## Acceptance criteria

- A meaningful visual regression suite exists (not just trivial snapshots).
- Snapshots are stable across machines/CI.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zuzg","title":"E2E: Table rendering complete scenarios","description":"End-to-end Table tests: (1) Simple table creation, (2) Styled headers/cells, (3) Column alignment, (4) Border styles, (5) Nested tables, (6) Large data sets, (7) Unicode content, (8) Width constraints. Compare against expected ANSI output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:50.229429445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:37:03.362347784Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:37:03.362285147Z","close_reason":"e2e_table.rs has 36 tests covering: table creation, styled headers/cells, column alignment, border styles, nested tables, large datasets, Unicode content, and width constraints.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zuzg","depends_on_id":"bd-20dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:54.665076736Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zuzg","depends_on_id":"bd-vh8c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:50.242527065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-zxwt","title":"Test flakiness: test_terminal_env_apply fails due to env var race","description":"The test common::platform::tests::test_terminal_env_apply in tests/common/platform.rs is flaky because it modifies environment variables (TERM, COLORTERM, etc.) without proper test isolation.

Symptom: When running cargo test with parallel test threads, the test sometimes fails.

Root Cause: Tests that modify process-global environment variables race with each other when run in parallel.

Solution: Add serial_test crate and mark env-modifying tests with #[serial].

Verification: Test passes with cargo test --test e2e_combined terminal_env_apply -- --test-threads=1","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T01:28:32.598617235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:31:14.291921557Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:31:14.291853660Z","close_reason":"Fixed by adding serial_test crate and #[serial] attribute to env-modifying tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zzss","title":"demo_showcase tests: non-interactive safety","description":"Add tests that specifically guard non-interactive safety (no hangs, no infinite output, no stuck cursor) with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedWaterfall","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:14.598189643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:38:38.673800898Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:38:38.673726429Z","close_reason":"Added 5 non-interactive safety tests: full demo completion, bounded output, no ANSI leakage, no cursor control, live auto-disabled. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zzss","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:05.531279556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zzss","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.262979447Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zzss","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:14.628106917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zzss","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:07.889954809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":71,"issue_id":"bd-zzss","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What this guards

- Running in pipes/non-TTY must never hang.
- Live/alt-screen should be auto-disabled unless forced.
- Output must be bounded (no infinite animation frames).

## Test approach

- Run the full demo (or a representative subset) in CI-safe mode with a hard timeout.
- Assert output length is within a sane upper bound (guard against runaway loops).

## Acceptance criteria

- A regression that would cause `... | cat` to hang fails the test with clear logs.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:04Z"},{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-zzss","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update

Standardize the safety run to include deterministic + bounded settings:
- `--quick`
- `--seed 0`
- `--color-system none`

This keeps runtime and output volume predictable.","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-01n","title":"Validate Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13","description":"## Objective
Verify the text wrapping implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 13 exactly.

## Background
Text wrapping is critical for responsive terminal output. The algorithm must handle word boundaries, long words, wide characters, and styled text spans correctly.

## Validation Checklist

### 13.1 Word Boundary Detection
- [ ] Verify words split on whitespace
- [ ] Verify hyphenated words treated as single unit (or split at hyphen)
- [ ] Verify CJK characters can wrap between any characters

### 13.2 Wrap Algorithm
- [ ] Verify words fit: add to current line
- [ ] Verify word too long: break at width (fold mode)
- [ ] Verify line break: start new line, preserve indent
- [ ] Verify whitespace at line break: consumed (not carried)

### 13.3 Overflow Methods
- [ ] Verify Fold: break anywhere when word > width
- [ ] Verify Crop: truncate at width
- [ ] Verify Ellipsis: truncate at width-1 and add ellipsis

### 13.4 Style Preservation
- [ ] Verify spans are split correctly across line breaks
- [ ] Verify style continues on wrapped lines
- [ ] Verify partial spans at wrap point

### 13.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test wrapping with wide characters (2-cell)
- [ ] Test wrapping with zero-width characters
- [ ] Test wrapping at exactly width boundary
- [ ] Test no_wrap flag
- [ ] Test empty text
- [ ] Test single long word

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (wrap methods)
- src/cells.rs (width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 13","acceptance_criteria":"All wrap scenarios work correctly. Styles preserved across line breaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:19:12.644260470Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","close_reason":"Validated Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13. All 41 E2E tests and 17 unit tests pass. Implementation correctly handles: word boundary detection via character iteration, wrap algorithm with proper line division, all 4 overflow methods (Fold, Crop, Ellipsis, Ignore), style preservation through slice()/divide(), and edge cases (empty text, whitespace, zero width, wide chars). No gaps found.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-01n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-02i","title":"Verify Markup Parser Error Recovery","description":"## Objective
Ensure markup parser gracefully handles malformed input as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.

## Background
Users will make mistakes in markup. The parser should render as much as possible and treat invalid markup as literal text rather than crashing.

## Technical Details

### Error Recovery Cases

#### Unclosed Tags
Input: '[bold]hello'
Expected: render 'hello' with bold, auto-close at end

#### Unmatched Close Tags
Input: 'hello[/bold]'
Expected: render '[/bold]' as literal text

#### Invalid Style in Tag
Input: '[invalidstyle]hello[/]'
Expected: render '[invalidstyle]hello[/]' as literal OR apply null style

#### Nested Mismatches
Input: '[bold][italic]hello[/bold][/italic]'
Expected: reasonable behavior (spec should define)

#### Empty Tags
Input: '[]hello'
Expected: render '[]hello' as literal OR ignore empty tag

#### Escaped Brackets
Input: 'price is \[100\]'
Expected: render 'price is [100]'

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each error case
2. Compare to Python Rich behavior
3. Document any intentional deviations
4. Ensure no panics/crashes on any input

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (parser)
- Python Rich markup.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.4","acceptance_criteria":"Parser handles all malformed input gracefully. No panics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:22:31.584465299Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T05:00:48.345989669Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T05:00:48.345989669Z","close_reason":"VERIFICATION COMPLETE: Markup parser error recovery fully implemented and tested.

Verified against RICH_SPEC.md Section 4:

1. **Unclosed Tags** (`[bold]hello`):
   - Lines 229-236: Auto-closes at end ✓
   - Test: test_render_unclosed_tag ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.5

2. **Unmatched Close Tags** (`hello[/bold]`):
   - Lines 199-214: Returns MarkupError::UnmatchedClosingTag ✓
   - Test: test_unmatched_closing_tag ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.6

3. **Implicit Close with Empty Stack** (`hello[/]`):
   - Line 207: Returns error ✓
   - Test: test_empty_close_nothing_to_close ✓

4. **Invalid Style in Tag** (`[invalidstyle]hello[/]`):
   - Line 268: Falls back to Style::new() gracefully ✓
   - Test: test_invalid_style_graceful ✓

5. **Empty Tags** (`[]hello`):
   - Regex requires [a-z#/@...], [] doesn't match ✓
   - Test: test_empty_tag_content ✓

6. **Escaped Brackets** (`\[100]`):
   - Lines 108, 122-128, 192-194 ✓
   - Tests: test_render_escaped_bracket, test_mixed_escaped_and_tags ✓
   - MATCHES SPEC Section 4.4

7. **Nested Mismatches**:
   - pop_matching() searches stack for explicit closes ✓
   - Test: test_nested_with_explicit_close ✓

8. **Graceful Fallback** (render_or_plain):
   - Lines 282-284: Returns plain text on error ✓
   - Test: test_render_or_plain_fallback ✓

9. **No Panic Tests**:
   - test_no_panic_on_random_brackets covers edge cases ✓
   - Unicode/emoji tests pass ✓

All 15 markup tests pass. Implementation is spec-compliant.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-qgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
